developer.comment: Built by AWS / Amazon; runs on Amazon Bedrock.

first-release.comment: Public preview announced 2025-07-14; reached general availability 2025-11-17.

status.comment: Generally available since 2025-11-17 (was in preview from July 2025). Checked 2026-06-02.

license.comment: Closed-source product; the public GitHub repo hosts issues/docs only, not the IDE source.

specialization.comment: Broad coding agent; spec-driven development is a methodology, not a domain niche, so kept general-purpose per the Specialization guideline.

interface.comment: Standalone desktop IDE plus a terminal CLI (added at GA) and a cloud/web autonomous agent at app.kiro.dev.

host-ides.comment: Standalone editor forked from Code OSS (VS Code); also a terminal CLI and a browser-based cloud agent.

open-source.comment: Editor is built on open-source Code OSS, but Kiro's own agent/IDE source is not openly licensed; the GitHub repo is issues/docs only.

self-contained-vs-plugin.comment: Ships as a standalone editor, a CLI tool, and a hosted cloud autonomous agent.

default-model-provider.comment: Flagship/default models are Anthropic Claude (Sonnet/Opus/Haiku); also offers open-weight models and an Auto router.

model-choice.comment: Model dropdown lets users switch among Claude variants, several open-weight models, and Auto.

bring-your-own-key.comment: Models are billed via Kiro's credit system on Amazon Bedrock; docs do not document supplying your own provider API key.

local-model-support.comment: No documented support for local/self-hosted models (Ollama, vLLM, etc.); models run through Bedrock.

multi-file-editing.comment: Autopilot makes coordinated changes across multiple files in a single task.

autonomous-task-execution.comment: Autopilot completes tasks end-to-end (creates/edits files, runs commands) with minimal per-step intervention.

terminal-command-execution.comment: Can run shell commands as part of its loop, with per-command trust/permission tiers.

mcp-support.comment: Documented Model Context Protocol support, including remote MCP at GA.

subagents-parallel-agents.comment: Built-in context-gathering and general-purpose subagents; supports running tasks in parallel, each with its own context window.

codebase-indexing.comment: Automatically indexes the codebase and docs for context-aware retrieval; re-indexes on file changes.

planning-mode.comment: Spec-driven workflow generates a requirements/design spec for review and approval before writing code — an explicit plan-then-execute phase.

multimodal-input.comment: Multimodal chat accepts images — screenshots, diagrams, schemas — as context.

browser-computer-use.comment: Has web tools for fetching URLs, but no documented ability to drive a real browser/GUI to act and verify changes. Marked null pending clear confirmation. Checked 2026-06-02.

operating-mode.comment: Interactive chat, supervised (per-action approval) and Autopilot autonomous modes in the IDE/CLI, plus an async cloud agent that runs detached in a sandbox. No tab-style inline completion.

inline-completion.comment: Positioned around spec-driven and agentic chat; no documented as-you-type tab autocomplete.

background-async-execution.comment: Cloud autonomous agent runs tasks asynchronously in isolated sandboxes (e.g. from a GitHub issue) while the developer does other work.

persistent-memory.comment: Steering files store persistent project knowledge (patterns, standards) that persist across sessions and are synced between IDE and CLI.

checkpoints-rollback.comment: Checkpointing (added at GA) lets you rewind/roll back agent execution steps; Autopilot changes can be reverted as a unit.

git-integration.comment: Autonomous agent creates feature branches, makes commits, and pushes; IDE is built on Code OSS with native git.

pr-creation.comment: Autonomous agent opens pull requests (via the Kiro Agent GitHub app or as your own user).

custom-rules-instructions.comment: Steering files provide project-level and global instructions (coding standards, preferred workflows).

hooks-extensibility.comment: Agent hooks fire on IDE events (file save/create/delete) to run predefined agent actions; plus MCP and custom subagents.

permission-model.comment: Per-request approval by default with configurable trust tiers; Autopilot can auto-run trusted commands/edits. Cloud agent runs in a sandbox.

self-host-on-prem.comment: No documented self-host/on-prem deployment; runs as a hosted service on Amazon Bedrock infrastructure.

privacy-mode.comment: Enterprise users' content is not used for service improvement; free/individual subscribers can opt out via telemetry/content toggles.

enterprise-controls.comment: SSO via AWS IAM Identity Center with group-based controls/permission sets and centralized team management. Audit logs/RBAC/VPC not clearly documented as Kiro features. Checked 2026-06-02.

pricing-model.comment: Free tier (50 credits) plus Pro/Pro+/Power subscriptions, with metered overage at $0.04/credit.

free-tier.comment: Free plan: 50 credits/month with open-weight models and Claude Sonnet 4.5.

entry-price-usd-mo.comment: Cheapest paid plan is Pro at $20/month (1,000 credits). Checked 2026-06-02.

maturity.comment: GA since Nov 2025 with team features and CLI, but a young product still rapidly adding capabilities; usable but evolving.

community-size.comment: AWS backing and high visibility, but a newer single-vendor tool; smaller than Cursor/Copilot scale. Checked 2026-06-02.

extension-plugin-ecosystem.comment: Built on Code OSS, supporting Open VSX-compatible extensions; plus MCP servers and custom subagents/hooks.

project-memory-files.comment: Auto-loads markdown steering files from .kiro/steering/ (vendor-specific convention → custom) and also picks up AGENTS.md automatically. No CLAUDE.md/.cursorrules/copilot-instructions convention. Checked 2026-06-02.

memory-scopes.comment: Workspace (project) steering in .kiro/steering/, global (user) steering in ~/.kiro/steering/, plus a team scope distributed via MDM/central repositories (enterprise). No per-folder/subdirectory scope documented.

scoped-rules-files.comment: Inclusion modes: 'always' (always-on), 'fileMatch' glob patterns (path-based), and 'auto' where Kiro pulls a file in when the request matches its description (agent-requested). A manual #-reference mode also exists for user-triggered inclusion.

auto-memory.comment: Kiro has no autonomous cross-session memory writing; context is captured through explicitly defined/managed steering files, not memories the agent writes on its own. Checked 2026-06-02.

skill-support.comment: Agent Skills (based on the open agentskills.io standard) are modular instruction packages loaded on demand; plus custom subagents that appear as slash commands.

skill-invocation.comment: Users invoke skills explicitly via '/' slash commands; Kiro also activates a skill autonomously when a request matches the skill's description.

always-on-surface.comment: Three-stage progressive disclosure: only skill names and descriptions load at startup; full instructions load on activation; scripts/reference files load on demand during execution.

skill-distribution.comment: Skills imported from GitHub (paste a repo/SKILL.md URL) or the local filesystem, then copied into .kiro/skills/ or ~/.kiro/skills/. Portable as community packages via the agentskills.io standard; no curated one-click marketplace documented. Checked 2026-06-02.
